Motion To Compel Jaremy Bolster'S Responses To Special Interrogatories, Nos. 36 - 80, 86 - 91, 97 - 100, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Joshua Ezrin, Esq. 415-557-2558 Ezrin Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Davis J. Reilly, Esq. (415) 981-5411 BLEDSOE, DIESTEL, TREPPA & CRANE for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1-DEFENDANT XYLO LLC, Motion To Compel Jaremy Bolster'S Responses To Special Interrogatories, Nos. 36 - 80, 86 - 91, 97 - 100, And For Monetary Sanctions.

The Motion is granted. Plaintiff must respond to the interrogatories in dispute within 20 days. Simply objecting solely on the grounds that the statute only authorizes thirty-five (35) interrogatories, as is the case here, is not sufficient. (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 2 (Rutter Group 1996) 8:956, p. 8F-14 ["The clear implication is that the responding party cannot simply object to more than 35 interrogatories. Rather, the responding party must seek a protective order (within the time and in the manner required by CCP 2030.090(a)].) Sanctions are awarded to Defendant in the amount of $2,056.30.

Mr. Reilly to submit two proposed orders approved as to form in word format directly to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2-DEFENDANT XYLO LLC's, Motion To Compel Jonathan Bolster'S Responses To Special Interrogatories, Nos. 36 - 80, 86 - 91, 97 - 100, And For Monetary Sanctions.

The Motion is granted. Plaintiff must respond to the interrogatories in dispute within 20 days. Simply objecting solely on the grounds the statute only authorizes thirty-five (35) interrogatories, as is the case here, is not sufficient. (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 2 (Rutter Group 1996) 8:956, p. 8F-14 ["The clear implication is that the responding party cannot simply object to more than 35 interrogatories. Rather, the responding party must seek a protective order (within the time and in the manner required by CCP 2030.090(a)].) No sanctions are awarded on this Motion in that it is essentially an exact duplicate of the accompanying Motion for which sanctions are awarded.

Mr. Reilly to submit two proposed orders approved as to form in word format directly to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
